demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis demographics; corrected the primary phone number from a landline to the daughter's mobile. Verified Straight Medicare coverage and confirmed the home address in the renal-friendly zip code matches the SNF face sheet.
home_vs_ltc_determination: SW confirms the goal is Home, though the patient's son is currently advocating for LTC placement due to the complexity of the dialysis regimen.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: SW reports the patient is clinically stable, but the discharge timeline is fluid due to the son's refusal to sign off on the home plan. PN requested a post-conference summary from the SW.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into the patient's tablet; 'Home Care vs. SNF' program flyer delivered to both the patient and the daughter during the bedside visit.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached the daughter (POA); she confirms the home is ready and she will be present for the transition. Son's concerns remain, but the SW has cleared the discharge to the daughter's care.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date of 05/19 and 1st visit date of 05/20 into the Atlantis tracking module.
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant visit scheduled for 05/20 at 09:00 AM, ensuring the 24-hour post-discharge window is met.
narrative_summary: Martha G. was identified in Atlantis as a renal failure transition requiring complex home monitoring. During the maintenance phase, a significant family disagreement emerged between the daughter (pro-home) and the son (pro-LTC), which I escalated to the SW for mediation. Once the SW finalized the home plan, I conducted the pulse call to verify logistics and successfully scheduled the MA for the morning after discharge to secure the first-visit incentive.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Family Disagreement on Discharge Plan regarding home dialysis safety.
HHA_SOC_Overlap: Difficulty finding a nurse certified for Martha's specific dialysis fistula care.
Friday Discharge Gap: Facility pushed discharge to Friday afternoon, risking a weekend without monitoring.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's son demands a cost-benefit analysis of home care versus a long-term care facility.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN educated the family on the program benefits and safety monitoring while deferring financial comparisons to the Social Worker.

format_2_triples.situation: Son threatens to block the transport company from taking the patient home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN flagged the volatile family situation to the Social Worker for immediate intervention and a formal family meeting.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA coordinator is uncertain if they can staff the Saturday morning visit for a Friday discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN verified HHA receipt of the referral and confirmed the assigned nurse name and first-visit time window for the MA to follow.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Step back from the family conflict to allow the Social Worker to lead the multidisciplinary meeting regarding the son's preference for LTC.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary and ensures the PN does not overstep into placement decisions or financial counseling.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Walk the daughter through the Day 1 expectations, specifically explaining who will arrive and how the MA will assist the HHA nurse in the 24-hour window.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the Stage 2 Patient Education checklist to reduce caregiver anxiety and ensure service adoption.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Provide the son with a detailed cost analysis showing that home care is more affordable than the 100-day private pay rate at a local LTC facility.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the Banned Actions regarding providing cost analysis and handling financial cliff discussions.
